turns.content: What brings you in today?

I have this pressure in the middle of my chest since I woke up this morning. It feels like something heavy is sitting on my chest. It comes and goes but it's been there all day. It's very strong, and I'm also a little short of breath.
When did this start?
It started this morning, around 7:30, right after I got out of bed. I was just making coffee when I felt this pressure in my chest. It hasn't really gone away since then, just keeps coming and going.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

It's right in the center of my chest, behind my breastbone. It feels like a heavy pressure, not a sharp pain. Sometimes it goes away for a few minutes, but then it comes back just as strong. I also feel a little dizzy and sick to my stomach.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, no fever or chills. Just the pressure in my chest, the trouble breathing, and feeling a little sick. That's what worries me the most - I've never had this before.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take lisinopril for my blood pressure, metformin for my diabetes, and also atorvastatin for cholesterol. I take them every day like my doctor told me. My wife helps me remember to take them.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

Yes, I have high blood pressure and type 2 diabetes. I also have a problem with my weight - my doctor says I'm obese. I've had these conditions for about eight years now. That's why I'm worried about this chest pressure, because I know it could be serious with those problems.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I'm not allergic to any medicines. My wife always asks about that, and I told her no. But the doctor did tell me once to be careful with aspirin because of my stomach. That's all I know.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

No, I just sat down and tried to rest a little, but it didn't help much. I usually take Tylenol for headaches, but I didn't think that would be right for this. My wife told me not to take anything before talking to the doctor. So I just came straight here.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Well, when I sit still, it feels a tiny bit better. But when I try to walk around or do anything, the pressure gets worse and I get more short of breath. Lying down on the couch didn't really help either. I can't figure out what makes it go away completely. I just feel worried because it's not stopping.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

I couldn't do anything today. I was supposed to go over some tax forms for a client, but I couldn't focus at all. My wife got worried and brought me here. I'm a very active person normally, but this chest pressure just stops me. I'm scared, to be honest.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

Yes, I used to smoke about a pack a day, but I cut down to maybe half a pack now. That's been for about thirty years, I guess. I also drink a little - maybe a beer or a glass of wine with dinner a few times a week. But never a lot, just enough to relax. My doctor told me to quit smoking completely, but it's hard.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

My father had a heart attack when he was around my age, maybe 58. He also had high blood pressure and diabetes, just like me. That's one of the reasons I'm so scared right now. My wife said I should come in because she remembered what happened to him.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, never. I've had some chest tightness before after a big meal or when I'm really stressed, but nothing like this. This pressure is so strong and it just won't stop. It feels completely different from before, and that's why I came to the emergency room.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, I think I understand. You're asking about my chest pressure, when it started, and what makes it worse or better. I've answered all that. But I'm still very worried about what's happening to me. The pressure is still there, and I'm scared it could be a heart attack like my father had. Can you help me?
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

I'd say the pain is about a 5, maybe even a 6 when it's at its worst. And emotionally? I'm really scared, so I'd say a 9. I can't stop thinking about my father and what happened to him. My wife is worried too. That's why I'm here.
Do you have any questions for me?

Yes, I have a question. Do you think this could be a heart attack? I'm really scared because of what happened to my father. And also, what are you going to do to check what's wrong with me? I want to know what tests you'll do, if you don't mind telling me simply.
What do you think might be going on?

I don't know for sure, but I'm very worried it could be a heart attack. The pressure in my chest feels like what I've heard about, and with my father's history, I just feel scared. That's why I came here - I need you to tell me what's really happening.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

I think that's everything important. Just to mention, I was planning to drive myself to work this morning, but I felt so dizzy and the chest pressure got worse, so I turned back home. My wife brought me here right away. That's why I'm so worried - it stopped me from doing normal things.
